title: dryopteris flexilis
intro: Dryopteris flexilis, also known as western goldenback fern, is a species of fern native to the western regions of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for wound care and skin irritations. The health benefits of the western goldenback fern include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve respiratory function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can be dried and made into capsules or tablets for oral consumption.
